arguments.command: cat schema.md

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
In the past year, is there any month when we received significantly more cases for Women's Trail Running Shorts compared to other months? The associated product Id is 01tWs000002wSKYIA2. Return only the month name.

# Task Instructions
- Today's date: 2022-08-28

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

arguments.command: python3 query.py "SELECT strftime('%m', c.CreatedDate) AS mon, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM \"Case\" c JOIN OrderItem oi ON c.OrderItemId__c = oi.Id WHERE oi.Product2Id = '01tWs000002wSKYIA2' AND c.CreatedDate >= '2021-08-28' AND c.CreatedDate <= '2022-08-28' GROUP BY mon ORDER BY cnt DESC"

If you prefer not to keep the incorrect item and would rather receive your money back, you can opt for a Full Refund. This solution provides a complete refund for the purchase amount, including any shipping fees incurred. The Full Refund option is typically used when the items received are either incorrect or damaged.

To qualify for a Full Refund, you must initiate the return process within 30 days from the purchase date. Here are the steps to follow:
- Contact our customer service team through our official website or call our toll-free customer support line.
- Provide your order number, a description of the incorrect item received, and any relevant photos to expedite the process.
- Our team will issue a return authorization and provide instructions on how to return the incorrect item.
- Once we receive the returned item, we will process your refund immediately. The refund should appear in your account within 5-7 business days depending on your banking institution.

2. Replacement:

# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

task:
Find the agent with the shortest handle time who managed over 2 cases in February 2021. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).

- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

## Today's date: 2022-11-24

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH firstassign AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, NewValue__c AS agent,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CaseId__c ORDER BY CreatedDate ASC) AS rn
  FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment'
),
creation AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, CreatedDate FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Case Creation'
)
SELECT fa.agent, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM firstassign fa
JOIN creation c ON c.CaseId__c = fa.CaseId__c
WHERE fa.rn=1 AND c.CreatedDate >= '2021-02-01' AND c.CreatedDate < '2021-03-01'
GROUP BY fa.agent
HAVING COUNT(*) > 2
ORDER BY n DESC"

task:
Who had the shortest average handle time among agents who handled more than 3 cases in the past 6 months? Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2023-10-05

## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).

- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

Who had the shortest average handle time among agents who handled more than 3 cases in the past 6 months? Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Time Periods

- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.

## Today's date: 2023-10-05

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH firstowner AS (
  SELECT ch.CaseId__c AS cid, ch.NewValue__c AS agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c ch
  WHERE ch.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
  AND ch.CreatedDate = (SELECT MIN(ch2.CreatedDate) FROM CaseHistory__c ch2 WHERE ch2.CaseId__c=ch.CaseId__c AND ch2.Field__c='Owner Assignment')
),
oacount AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c AS cid, COUNT(*) AS n
  FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
)
SELECT fo.agent,
  COUNT(*) AS cases_in_period
FROM firstowner fo
JOIN \"Case\" c ON c.Id = fo.cid
WHERE c.CreatedDate >= '2023-04-05' AND c.CreatedDate < '2023-10-06'
GROUP BY fo.agent
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3
ORDER BY cases_in_period DESC
"

    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CaseHistory__c ch3 WHERE ch3.CaseId__c=ch.CaseId__c AND ch3.Field__c='Owner Assignment') AS oacnt
  FROM CaseHistory__c ch
  WHERE ch.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
  AND ch.CreatedDate = (SELECT MIN(ch2.CreatedDate) FROM CaseHistory__c ch2 WHERE ch2.CaseId__c=ch.CaseId__c AND ch2.Field__c='Owner Assignment')
)
SELECT fo.agent, COUNT(*) total,
  SUM(CASE WHEN fo.oacnt=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) non_transferred
FROM firstowner fo
JOIN \"Case\" c ON c.Id = fo.cid
WHERE c.CreatedDate >= '2023-04-05' AND c.CreatedDate <= '2023-10-05'
GROUP BY fo.agent HAVING total>3
"

task:
Who had the shortest average handle time among agents who handled more than 3 cases in the past 6 months? Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

task:
Use the case routing policy to determine the most suitable agent for the given case. Return only the Id of the agent.
## Given Case
- Case Subject: Received Incorrect Item - Urgent Exchange Needed
- Case Description: I recently placed an order for the Women's High-Performance Jacket, but I received a completely different item instead. I urgently need an exchange for the correct item as soon as possible. Please assist me with this issue promptly.

# Task Instructions
## Case Routing Policy
The case routing policy determines the best agent to assign the given new case based on the following criteria
- Issue Expertise: The agent who has closed the most cases associated with the issue most similar to the given case.
- Product Expertise: If there is a tie in issue expertise, the best agent is the one who has solved the most cases associated with the product most relevant to the given case.
- Workload: If there is still a tie, the best agent is the one that has least cases with Status not 'Closed'.

observation.content:
[
  {
    "Id": "ka0Ws000000Qrn3IAC",
    "Title": "Navigating Warranty Claims for Defective Equipment at Shoes & Clothings",
    "FAQ_Answer__c": "Navigating Warranty Claims for Defective Equipment at Shoes & Clothings

Experiencing issues with defective equipment can be frustrating, but Shoes & Clothings is committed to ensuring that our customers are satisfied with their purchases. We provide two primary solutions for handling warranty claims related to defective equipment: replacement and extended warranty. Here’s a comprehensive guide to help you navigate the process with ease.

Solution: Replacement

If you receive a defective or damaged product from Shoes & Clothings, our Replacement solution is designed to provide a prompt resolution. This solution allows us to send a new replacement product to the customer when the original item is found to be defective upon receipt. To qualify for a replacement, the following conditions must be met:
- The claim must be made within 60 days from the date of purchase.
- The defective product must be returned to Shoes & Clothings, as per our return policy.

To initiate a replacement claim, follow these steps:

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

# Task Instructions
- Today's date: 2024-01-01

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

arguments.command: python3 query.py "SELECT substr(c.CreatedDate,1,7) as ym, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM \"Case\" c JOIN OrderItem oi ON c.OrderItemId__c = oi.Id WHERE oi.Product2Id = '01tWs000002wRDCIA2' GROUP BY ym ORDER BY ym"

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Find the agent who in December 2020 managed over three cases and had the highest number of transfers. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2021-08-20

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

arguments.command: python3 query.py "SELECT Field__c, COUNT(*) FROM CaseHistory__c GROUP BY Field__c"

task:
Find the agent who in December 2020 managed over three cases and had the highest number of transfers. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Which agent had the longest average handle time over the past 6 weeks for handling more than 1 case? Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH oa AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, COUNT(*) cnt, MIN(CreatedDate) firstassign
  FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
),
firstagent AS (
  SELECT h.CaseId__c, h.NewValue__c agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c h
  JOIN oa ON oa.CaseId__c=h.CaseId__c AND oa.firstassign=h.CreatedDate
  WHERE h.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
)
SELECT fa.agent,
  COUNT(*) ncases,
  AVG((julianday(c.ClosedDate)-julianday(c.CreatedDate))*24*60) avg_minutes
FROM \"Case\" c
JOIN oa ON oa.CaseId__c=c.Id
JOIN firstagent fa ON fa.CaseId__c=c.Id
WHERE oa.cnt=1
  AND c.ClosedDate IS NOT NULL
  AND c.ClosedDate >= '2021-10-06' AND c.ClosedDate <= '2021-11-17T23:59:59Z'
GROUP BY fa.agent
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY avg_minutes DESC
LIMIT 5"

task:
Which agent had the longest average handle time over the past 6 weeks for handling more than 1 case? Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH oa AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, COUNT(*) cnt, MIN(CreatedDate) firstassign
  FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
),
firstagent AS (
  SELECT h.CaseId__c, h.NewValue__c agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c h
  JOIN oa ON oa.CaseId__c=h.CaseId__c AND oa.firstassign=h.CreatedDate
  WHERE h.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
)
SELECT fa.agent, c.Id, oa.cnt
FROM \"Case\" c
JOIN oa ON oa.CaseId__c=c.Id
JOIN firstagent fa ON fa.CaseId__c=c.Id
WHERE c.ClosedDate >= '2021-10-06' AND c.ClosedDate <= '2021-11-18'"

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH oa AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, MIN(CreatedDate) firstassign
  FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
),
firstagent AS (
  SELECT h.CaseId__c, h.NewValue__c agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c h
  JOIN oa ON oa.CaseId__c=h.CaseId__c AND oa.firstassign=h.CreatedDate
  WHERE h.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
)
SELECT fa.agent, COUNT(*) n
FROM \"Case\" c
JOIN firstagent fa ON fa.CaseId__c=c.Id
WHERE c.ClosedDate >= '2021-10-06' AND c.ClosedDate <= '2021-11-18'
GROUP BY fa.agent HAVING COUNT(*)>1"

arguments.command: cat schema.md

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

# Task Instructions
## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. ## Time Periods

- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH firstassign AS (
  SELECT h.CaseId__c, h.NewValue__c as agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c h
  WHERE h.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
  AND h.CreatedDate = (SELECT MIN(h2.CreatedDate) FROM CaseHistory__c h2 WHERE h2.CaseId__c=h.CaseId__c AND h2.Field__c='Owner Assignment')
),
oacount AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, COUNT(*) c FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
)
SELECT fa.agent,
  COUNT(*) as managed_cases,
  SUM(CASE WHEN oc.c=1 THEN (julianday(c.ClosedDate)-julianday(c.CreatedDate))*86400 ELSE 0 END) as total_ht_sec,
  SUM(CASE WHEN oc.c=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as ht_cases
FROM \"Case\" c
JOIN firstassign fa ON fa.CaseId__c=c.Id
JOIN oacount oc ON oc.CaseId__c=c.Id
WHERE c.ClosedDate >= '2022-06-05' AND c.ClosedDate <= '2022-07-03T23:59:59Z'
GROUP BY fa.agent
ORDER BY fa.agent
"

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH firstassign AS (
  SELECT h.CaseId__c, h.NewValue__c as agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c h
  WHERE h.Field__c='Owner Assignment'
  AND h.CreatedDate = (SELECT MIN(h2.CreatedDate) FROM CaseHistory__c h2 WHERE h2.CaseId__c=h.CaseId__c AND h2.Field__c='Owner Assignment')
),
oacount AS (
  SELECT CaseId__c, COUNT(*) c FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY CaseId__c
),
windowcases AS (
  SELECT c.Id, fa.agent, oc.c as oa,
    (julianday(substr(c.ClosedDate,1,19))-julianday(substr(c.CreatedDate,1,19)))*86400 as ht
  FROM \"Case\" c
  JOIN firstassign fa ON fa.CaseId__c=c.Id
  JOIN oacount oc ON oc.CaseId__c=c.Id
  WHERE c.ClosedDate >= '2022-06-05' AND c.ClosedDate <= '2022-07-03T23:59:59Z'
),
managed_all AS (
  SELECT agent, COUNT(*) mc FROM firstassign GROUP BY agent
)
SELECT w.agent, ma.mc as managed_all_time,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN w.oa=1 THEN 1 END) as ht_case_count,
  SUM(CASE WHEN w.oa=1 THEN w.ht END) as total_ht,
  AVG(CASE WHEN w.oa=1 THEN w.ht END) as avg_ht
FROM windowcases w
JOIN managed_all ma ON ma.agent=w.agent
GROUP BY w.agent
HAVING managed_all_time > 2
ORDER BY total_ht ASC
"

arguments.command: cat schema.md

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

task:
Find the agent with the lowest transfer counts who handled over 3 cases in the winter of 2021. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2024-10-05

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter: December 1 to February 28/29 (both inclusive).
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Query the CRM database to determine the answer, then submit EXACTLY what the question asks for and nothing else (an Id, a value, or 'None' when no record applies).

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
SELECT NewValue__c agent, COUNT(*) cnt
FROM CaseHistory__c
WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND OldValue__c IS NULL
AND CreatedDate >= '2021-12-01' AND CreatedDate < '2022-03-01'
GROUP BY NewValue__c
HAVING cnt > 3
ORDER BY cnt DESC"

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Use the case routing policy to determine the most suitable agent for the given case. Return only the Id of the agent.
## Given Case
- Case Subject: Incorrect Item Received - Dynamic Run Shorts
- Case Description: I ordered the Dynamic Run Shorts but received a completely different item instead. I need to exchange it for the correct shorts as soon as possible. Please assist me with this issue immediately.

# Task Instructions
## Case Routing Policy
The case routing policy determines the best agent to assign the given new case based on the following criteria
- Issue Expertise: The agent who has closed the most cases associated with the issue most similar to the given case.
- Product Expertise: If there is a tie in issue expertise, the best agent is the one who has solved the most cases associated with the product most relevant to the given case.
- Workload: If there is still a tie, the best agent is the one that has least cases with Status not 'Closed'.

observation.content:
[
  {
    "Id": "ka0Ws000000QrlRIAS",
    "Title": "How to Handle Order Cancellation Issues with Shoes & Clothings",
    "FAQ_Answer__c": "Handling Order Cancellation Issues with Shoes & Clothings

If you encounter difficulties canceling your order on the Shoes & Clothings website, rest assured there are effective solutions available. Below, we outline the steps you can take to address this issue and ensure a smooth resolution.

1. **Check Cancellation Window**: Verify that your order is indeed within the cancellation window. Orders are typically eligible for cancellation within a specific time frame after the purchase is made. This information can usually be found in your order confirmation email or on your Shoes & Clothings account.

2. **Full Refund**: If you are unable to cancel your order online but are still within 30 days of the purchase date, you may be eligible for a full refund. This solution is ideal if the items you ordered are damaged, incorrect, or otherwise unsatisfactory. To pursue a full refund, contact our customer service team with your order number and details about the issue. Our team will process the refund, including any shipping fees, making sure you are reimbursed promptly.

3. **Store Credit**: Alternatively, you may opt for store credit, which can be a convenient way to manage future purchases if you decide not to get a direct refund. This solution is available within 90 days of the purchase date and will be credited to your Shoes & Clothings account. Store credit is valid for future purchases and can be a flexible option if you plan to shop with us again. To request store credit, reach out to customer service with your order information, and they will facilitate the credit transfer to your account.

4. **Contacting Customer Service**: If you are within the eligible time frames for either a full refund (30 days) or store credit (90 days), and find yourself unable to cancel the order online, direct communication with our customer service is key. Provide them with your order details, including the order number, date of purchase, and the reason for cancellation. ...[truncated]",
    "Summary": "Learn how to effectively manage an order cancellation issue on the Shoes & Clothings website. If you find yourself unable to cancel your order online within the stipulated cancellation window, this article provides you with feasible solutions including full refunds and store credits. Understand the steps you can take to resolve the issue and ensure a smooth shopping experience."
  },
  {
    "Id": "ka0Ws000000QscfIAC",
    "Title": "Efficient Cancelation Policy for Orders at Shoes & Clothings",
    "FAQ_Answer__c": "Cancelling an order at Shoes & Clothings is a streamlined process designed to ensure utmost convenience for our valued customers. Follow these guidelines to understand the time-sensitive steps, qualifying conditions, and how to obtain a refund or store credit efficiently.

**Time-Sensitive Steps:**
To cancel an order, it’s crucial to act swiftly. You can cancel your order within a limited window after placing it, typically within the first 24 hours. This window may vary slightly depending on the specific items and current holiday or promotional schedules. We recommend checking your order confirmation email for precise details regarding cancellation windows.

**Qualifying Conditions:**
Not all orders qualify for cancellation. Custom items, such as personalized products, may not be eligible due to the bespoke nature of these goods. Furthermore, orders that have already been processed for shipping cannot be canceled. If your order does not qualify for cancellation but you still wish to return the item, our standard return policy will apply.

**Cancellation Process:**
To begin the cancellation process, follow these steps:
1. **Visit Our Website**: Log into your account on the Shoes & Clothings website.
2. **Access Order History**: Navigate to your order history to find the specific order you wish to cancel.
3. **Request Cancellation**: Click on the order and select the cancellation option. You will be prompted to confirm your decision.
4. **Confirmation Email**: Upon successfully submitting a cancellation request, you will receive a confirmation email outlining the details of your canceled order.

If you encounter any issues or your order does not appear eligible for cancellation, please reach out to our customer service team for assistance.

**Customer Service Contact Points:**
Our dedicated customer service team is available to help you with any cancellation inquiries. You can reach us through multiple channels:
- **Phone**: Call our customer service line for ...[truncated]",

The article aims to simplify the cancellation process and highlight customer service contact points for assistance."

observation.content:
Handling Order Cancellation Issues with Shoes & Clothings

If you encounter difficulties canceling your order on the Shoes & Clothings website, rest assured there are effective solutions available. Below, we outline the steps you can take to address this issue and ensure a smooth resolution.

1. **Check Cancellation Window**: Verify that your order is indeed within the cancellation window. Orders are typically eligible for cancellation within a specific time frame after the purchase is made. This information can usually be found in your order confirmation email or on your Shoes & Clothings account.

2. **Full Refund**: If you are unable to cancel your order online but are still within 30 days of the purchase date, you may be eligible for a full refund. This solution is ideal if the items you ordered are damaged, incorrect, or otherwise unsatisfactory. To pursue a full refund, contact our customer service team with your order number and details about the issue. Our team will process the refund, including any shipping fees, making sure you are reimbursed promptly.

3. **Store Credit**: Alternatively, you may opt for store credit, which can be a convenient way to manage future purchases if you decide not to get a direct refund. This solution is available within 90 days of the purchase date and will be credited to your Shoes & Clothings account. Store credit is valid for future purchases and can be a flexible option if you plan to shop with us again. To request store credit, reach out to customer service with your order information, and they will facilitate the credit transfer to your account.

4. **Contacting Customer Service**: If you are within the eligible time frames for either a full refund (30 days) or store credit (90 days), and find yourself unable to cancel the order online, direct communication with our customer service is key. Provide them with your order details, including the order number, date of purchase, and the reason for cancellation. ...[truncated]

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts over the past 3 quarters who has handled more than 3 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH handled AS (
  SELECT NewValue__c agent, COUNT(*) h FROM CaseHistory__c
  WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND OldValue__c IS NULL
  GROUP BY NewValue__c HAVING h > 3
),
transfers AS (
  SELECT OldValue__c agent, COUNT(*) t FROM CaseHistory__c
  WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND OldValue__c IS NOT NULL
    AND CreatedDate >= '2023-11-22' AND CreatedDate <= '2024-08-22'
  GROUP BY OldValue__c
)
SELECT h.agent, h.h handled_alltime, COALESCE(t.t,0) transfers_window
FROM handled h LEFT JOIN transfers t ON h.agent=t.agent
ORDER BY transfers_window ASC, handled_alltime DESC
"

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows

Addressing the wear and tear of yoga gear is crucial for ensuring it remains effective and safe to use. Inspect your mat, straps, and blocks regularly for signs of wear. If the mat starts peeling or losing its grip, it may be time to invest in a new one. Replace any straps that show fraying or compromised D-rings. For blocks, check for cracks or significant dents that could affect their stability.

In addition to maintaining your gear, practicing proper storage can extend its lifespan. Store your yoga mat rolled up and away from direct sunlight, which can cause the material to break down. Keep your straps and blocks in a cool, dry place to maintain their integrity.

By following these maintenance tips and addressing common issues promptly, you can ensure that your Shoes & Clothings yoga gear remains in optimal condition, allowing you to focus on your practice and perform at your best. Investing in high-quality, eco-friendly products and taking care of them ensures a safe, comfortable, and sustainable yoga experience.

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Use the case routing policy to determine the most suitable agent for the given case. Return only the Id of the agent.
## Given Case
- Case Subject: Size Mismatch of ZenPro Yoga Mat
- Case Description: I recently purchased the ZenPro Yoga Mat and found that the size chart on your website does not accurately reflect the actual size of the product. The mat is smaller than I expected and does not meet my needs.

# Task Instructions
## Case Routing Policy
The case routing policy determines the best agent to assign the given new case based on the following criteria
- Issue Expertise: The agent who has closed the most cases associated with the issue most similar to the given case.
- Product Expertise: If there is a tie in issue expertise, the best agent is the one who has solved the most cases associated with the product most relevant to the given case.
- Workload: If there is still a tie, the best agent is the one that has least cases with Status not 'Closed'.

task:
Identify the agent with the highest number of transfers in the last 4 months among those who have managed more than one case. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2022-02-27

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH first_assign AS (
  SELECT ch.CaseId__c, ch.NewValue__c AS agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c ch
  WHERE ch.Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND ch.OldValue__c IS NULL
),
managed AS (
  SELECT agent, COUNT(*) AS c FROM first_assign GROUP BY agent HAVING COUNT(*)>1
),
transfers AS (
  SELECT OldValue__c AS agent, COUNT(*) AS tcount
  FROM CaseHistory__c
  WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND OldValue__c IS NOT NULL
    AND CreatedDate >= '2021-10-27'
  GROUP BY OldValue__c
)
SELECT t.agent, t.tcount, m.c AS managed
FROM transfers t
JOIN managed m ON m.agent = t.agent

LIMIT 10
"

task:
Identify the agent with the highest number of transfers in the last 4 months among those who have managed more than one case. Return only the Id of the agent.

# Task Instructions
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to the first agent that the case was first assigned to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

arguments.command:
python3 query.py "
WITH first_assign AS (
  SELECT ch.CaseId__c, ch.NewValue__c AS agent
  FROM CaseHistory__c ch
  WHERE ch.Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND ch.OldValue__c IS NULL
),
managed AS (
  SELECT agent, COUNT(*) AS c FROM first_assign GROUP BY agent HAVING COUNT(*)>1
),
transfers AS (
  SELECT OldValue__c AS agent, COUNT(*) AS tcount
  FROM CaseHistory__c
  WHERE Field__c='Owner Assignment' AND OldValue__c IS NOT NULL
    AND CreatedDate >= '2021-10-27' AND CreatedDate <= '2022-02-27T23:59:59Z'
  GROUP BY OldValue__c
)
SELECT t.agent, t.tcount, m.c AS managed
FROM transfers t
JOIN managed m ON m.agent = t.agent
ORDER BY t.tcount DESC
LIMIT 10
"

observation.content:
# CRM database (crm.db)

A realistic Salesforce org as a read-only SQLite database. Query it with:

    python3 query.py "SELECT Id, Subject, Status FROM \"Case\" WHERE Status = 'Closed' LIMIT 5"

Notes:
- `Case`, `Order`, `User` are SQL reserved-ish words — wrap table names in double quotes.
- Ids are Salesforce ids (e.g. `005Ws000001xSR9IAM`). Foreign keys follow Salesforce naming:
  a column `FooId` or `FooId__c` references the `Foo` object's `Id` (e.g. `Case.ContactId` ->
  `Contact.Id`, `Case.OwnerId` -> `User.Id`, `CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c` -> `Case.Id`).
- Dates are ISO strings (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`); compare/sort them as text.

## Tables
### Account — 201 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), PersonEmail (TEXT), Phone (REAL), RecordTypeId (TEXT), ShippingCity (TEXT), ShippingState (TEXT)

### Case — 977 rows
Id (TEXT), Priority (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), Description (TEXT), Status (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), ClosedDate (TEXT), OrderItemId__c (TEXT), IssueId__c (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### CaseHistory__c — 2983 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId__c (TEXT), OldValue__c (TEXT), NewValue__c (TEXT), CreatedDate (TEXT), Field__c (TEXT)

### Contact — 208 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT)

### EmailMessage — 3241 rows
Id (TEXT), Subject (TEXT), TextBody (TEXT), ParentId (TEXT), FromAddress (TEXT), ToIds (TEXT), MessageDate (TEXT)

### Issue__c — 10 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT)

### Knowledge__kav — 45 rows
Id (TEXT), Title (TEXT), FAQ_Answer__c (TEXT), Summary (TEXT), UrlName (TEXT)

### LiveChatTranscript — 774 rows
Id (TEXT), CaseId (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), OwnerId (TEXT), Body (TEXT), EndTime (TEXT), LiveChatVisitorId (TEXT), ContactId (TEXT)

### Order — 2069 rows
Id (TEXT), AccountId (TEXT), Status (TEXT), EffectiveDate (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT)

### OrderItem — 7100 rows
Id (TEXT), OrderId (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), Quantity (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT), PriceBookEntryId (TEXT)

### Pricebook2 — 46 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), ValidFrom (TEXT), ValidTo (TEXT)

### PricebookEntry — 22000 rows
Id (TEXT), Pricebook2Id (TEXT), Product2Id (TEXT), UnitPrice (TEXT)

### Product2 — 501 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), Description (TEXT), IsActive (INTEGER), External_ID__c (TEXT)

### ProductCategory — 12 rows
Id (TEXT), Name (TEXT), CatalogId (TEXT)

### ProductCategoryProduct — 762 rows
Id (TEXT), ProductCategoryId (TEXT), ProductId (TEXT)

### User — 152 rows
Id (TEXT), FirstName (TEXT), LastName (TEXT), Email (TEXT), Phone (REAL), Username (TEXT), Alias (TEXT), LanguageLocaleKey (TEXT), EmailEncodingKey (TEXT), TimeZoneSidKey (TEXT), LocaleSidKey (TEXT)

task:
Use the case routing policy to determine the most suitable agent for the given case. Return only the Id of the agent.
## Given Case
- Case Subject: Confusion regarding return policy for Hiking Waterproof Boots
- Case Description: I purchased the Hiking Waterproof Boots online but I am a bit confused about the return policy conditions. Can someone clarify under what circumstances I can return these boots if they don't meet my expectations?

# Task Instructions
## Case Routing Policy
The case routing policy determines the best agent to assign the given new case based on the following criteria
- Issue Expertise: The agent who has closed the most cases associated with the issue most similar to the given case.
- Product Expertise: If there is a tie in issue expertise, the best agent is the one who has solved the most cases associated with the product most relevant to the given case.
- Workload: If there is still a tie, the best agent is the one that has least cases with Status not 'Closed'.

- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).

- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.
